mnemonics.title: Read vs Write Direction
mnemonics.content: Read = Receive (processor Receives data from memory). Write = Wield (processor Wields/sends data to memory). R ← [LOC] arrow points to register (Read). [LOC] ← R arrow points to memory (Write).

mnemonics.title: Memory Hierarchy Speed Order
mnemonics.content: R-C-M-S: Registers (fastest, ~1ns) → Cache (~5-10ns) → Main Memory (~50-100ns) → Secondary Storage (μs-ms, slowest). Remember: 'Really Cool Memory Systems' — each level is slower but larger and cheaper.

mnemonics.title: Instruction Execution Memory Accesses
mnemonics.content: F-O-S: Fetch instruction, get Operand, Store result. Every instruction starts with Fetch. Not every instruction needs all three — some skip Operand fetch or result Store.
